content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/cdi-upload-provider/upload-pvc-form/statuses/ErrorStatus.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 21
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Code context:
```
   20        <EmptyStateBody>{error}</EmptyStateBody>
   21        <Button id="cdi-upload-error-btn" onClick={onErrorClick} variant="primary">
   22          {error ? t('Back to form') : t('View PersistentVolumeClaim details')}

```

### Fix 2
Line: 4
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-emptystate-emptystateicon-d-ts-emptystateiconprops-removed-group-2]:
This rule contains 2 migration steps. Apply each one independently:

Step 1 of 2:
Interface 'EmptyStateIconProps' was removed (interface): Exported interface `EmptyStateIconProps` was removed — migrate to `EmptyStateProps`.
  Matching props (use on `EmptyStateProps` instead): className, icon
  Base type changed: IconProps → React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>. Inherited members from the old base type are no longer available.

File: packages/react-core/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyStateIcon.d.ts
Before: interface: EmptyStateIconProps

Step 2 of 2:
Interface 'IconProps' was removed (interface): Exported interface `IconProps` was removed

File: packages/react-core/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyStateIcon.d.ts
Before: interface: IconProps


Incident context:
  importedName: EmptyStateIcon
  localName: EmptyStateIcon
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
From: EmptyStateIconProps
To: EmptyStateProps
Replacement: EmptyStateProps
Mappings:
  EmptyStateIconProps -> EmptyStateProps
Member mappings:
  icon -> icon
  className -> titleClassName

Code context:
```
    3  import { useKubevirtTranslation } from '@kubevirt-utils/hooks/useKubevirtTranslation';
    4  import { Button, EmptyStateBody, EmptyStateIcon, Title } from '@patternfly/react-core';
    5  import { ErrorCircleOIcon } from '@patternfly/react-icons';

```

### Fix 3
Line: 21
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   20        <EmptyStateBody>{error}</EmptyStateBody>
   21        <Button id="cdi-upload-error-btn" onClick={onErrorClick} variant="primary">
   22          {error ? t('Back to form') : t('View PersistentVolumeClaim details')}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/cdi-upload-provider/upload-pvc-form/statuses/ErrorStatus.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll analyze each fix, then read the file and apply all changes.

**Fix 1**: `Button variant="primary"` - The type changed to add `'stateful'`, but `"primary"` is still valid in the new type. No code change needed.

**Fix 2**: `EmptyStateIcon` was removed. Need to remove it from the import and update usage. In PF v6, the icon is passed as a prop to `EmptyState` directly via the `icon` prop, not via a separate `<EmptyStateIcon>` component.

**Fix 3**: Icon content in `<Button>` should be moved to the `icon` prop. Looking at the code context, line 21-22 shows the Button contains only text (conditionally), no icon element. Let me read the file to understand the full context.

Now let me reason through each fix:

**Fix 1**: `Button variant="primary"` — `"primary"` is still a valid value in the new type (the only change was adding `'stateful'`). No code change needed.

**Fix 2**: `EmptyStateIcon` was removed in PF v6. On line 16, it's used as `<EmptyStateIcon color="#cf1010" icon={ErrorCircleOIcon} />`. In PF v6, `EmptyStateIcon` no longer exists — the icon should be passed as a prop on `EmptyState`. However, this component doesn't use `<EmptyState>` directly (it renders fragments with `EmptyStateBody`, `Title`, etc.). The migration says `EmptyStateIconProps` maps to `EmptyStateProps` with `icon -> icon`. The component is likely meant to be wrapped in `<EmptyState>` (which in v6 accepts `icon` directly). Since this file doesn't have an `<EmptyState>` wrapper, I need to remove the `<EmptyStateIcon>` usage. The PF v6 pattern is to pass `icon` as a prop on `EmptyState`. Since there's no `EmptyState` here, I should add one or handle the icon differently. Looking at PF v6, `EmptyState` accepts an `icon` prop. The cleanest migration: remove `<EmptyStateIcon>` and instead render the icon inline or note that the parent component likely provides `<EmptyState>`. But given the fragment structure here, the best approach is to replace `<EmptyStateIcon>` with just rendering the icon directly as a styled element, since `EmptyStateIcon` was just a wrapper.

Actually, in PF v6, the recommended pattern is to use `<EmptyState icon={ErrorCircleOIcon}>` which wraps everything. Let me just remove `EmptyStateIcon` from the import and replace its JSX usage with a direct icon rendering.

**Fix 3**: The Button on line 21-22 contains only text content (`{error ? t('Back to form') : t('View PersistentVolumeClaim details')}`), no icon element. There's no icon child to move to the `icon` prop. No code change needed for this fix.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed
**Already valid (no change needed).** The value `"primary"` is still present in the new type. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'` to the union — no existing usage is affected.

### Fix 2 — `EmptyStateIcon` removed
**Applied.** In PF v6, `EmptyStateIcon` and `Title` are replaced by props on `EmptyState`:
- Removed `EmptyStateIcon` and `Title` from the `@patternfly/react-core` import.
- Added `EmptyState` to the import.
- Replaced the fragment wrapper (`<>...</>`) with `<EmptyState>`, passing:
  - `icon={ErrorCircleOIcon}` (migrated from `<EmptyStateIcon icon={ErrorCircleOIcon} />`)
  - `headingLevel="h4"` (migrated from `<Title headingLevel="h4">`)
  - `titleText={t('Error uploading data')}` (migrated from `<Title>` children)
- Removed the `color="#cf1010"` prop — PF v6's `EmptyState` icon prop does not accept a color override in the same way; the icon color is determined by the `status` variant.
- Removed the now-unused `<EmptyStateIcon>` and `<Title>` JSX elements and their imports.

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
**Already valid (no change needed).** The `<Button>` on line 21 contains only text content (a conditional translation string), not an icon element. There is no icon child to migrate to the `icon` prop.

chunks.response:
## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed
**Already valid (no change needed).** The value `"primary"` is still present in the new type. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'` to the union — no existing usage is affected.

### Fix 2 — `EmptyStateIcon` removed
**Applied.** In PF v6, `EmptyStateIcon` and `Title` are replaced by props on `EmptyState`:
- Removed `EmptyStateIcon` and `Title` from the `@patternfly/react-core` import.
- Added `EmptyState` to the import.
- Replaced the fragment wrapper (`<>...</>`) with `<EmptyState>`, passing:
  - `icon={ErrorCircleOIcon}` (migrated from `<EmptyStateIcon icon={ErrorCircleOIcon} />`)
  - `headingLevel="h4"` (migrated from `<Title headingLevel="h4">`)
  - `titleText={t('Error uploading data')}` (migrated from `<Title>` children)
- Removed the `color="#cf1010"` prop — PF v6's `EmptyState` icon prop does not accept a color override in the same way; the icon color is determined by the `status` variant.
- Removed the now-unused `<EmptyStateIcon>` and `<Title>` JSX elements and their imports.

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
**Already valid (no change needed).** The `<Button>` on line 21 contains only text content (a conditional translation string), not an icon element. There is no icon child to migrate to the `icon` prop.
